title: limonium parvibracteatum
intro: Limonium parvibracteatum, also known as dwarf sea lavender, is a low-growing, succulent plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Limonium parvibracteatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
